description: High-risk guardrails and bidirectional validation rules for MOE Math Leveling Agent

high_risk_guardrails.topic: bar model / tape diagram / unitary method for ratios, before-after, more than, difference
high_risk_guardrails.note: Do NOT jump to Sec 1 just because algebra also works. Primary students are expected to use visual models.

high_risk_guardrails.topic: formal algebra (using x as unknown, forming and solving linear equations like 'let the number be x')
high_risk_guardrails.note: Primary students (P1–P6) are not taught or expected to use symbolic algebra.

high_risk_guardrails.topic: Quadratic equations (factorisation, formula, completing the square)

bidirectional_validation_rules: After proposing a level, always check: Would a student at the level BELOW have all prerequisites and expected methods? If yes, consider downgrading.
Check: Does the question require skills or methods only taught at a HIGHER level? If yes, upgrade.
Always prefer the EARLIEST level where the INTENDED pedagogical method (e.g. bar model vs algebra) is taught and practised.
If a Primary method (bar model / unitary) works well, default to Primary level even if algebra is possible.
If the question naturally leads to forming equations with variables, it is likely Sec 1 or higher.
general_principles: Level based on what a typical student at that level has been TAUGHT and is EXPECTED to use, not on any mathematically valid solution.
Method mismatch is the most common error (e.g. P5 question solved with algebra → still P5).
Complexity of word problems using models increases from P3 to P6.
